summary: Google Search remains the anchor of profitability, while Meta shows signs of audience fatigue and Bing suffers from a critical structural failure requiring immediate pause.

drivers.title: Search Outperforms PMax Efficiency
drivers.detail: Search_TM_Campaign_01 generates 17% of total portfolio revenue ($1.27M) at 6.32x ROAS with only 22% of Google's total spend. Any Q4 budget cut to this campaign would be catastrophically disproportionate to the savings achieved.

drivers.title: High Value Meta Campaigns
drivers.detail: Remarketing_Brand_Campaign_03 yields an exceptional 18.33x ROAS on $380K revenue, confirming it as the best risk-adjusted return vehicle in the portfolio.

risks.title: Meta Audience Saturation
risks.detail: Meta's overall ROAS has declined sharply from ~13x to ~6.5x over recent weeks, indicating heavy audience saturation that threatens future scalability.

risks.title: Bing Structural Failure
risks.detail: Bing ROAS collapsed below 0.1x in Feb-Mar 2026, with 22 of 28 campaigns producing absolutely zero revenue despite continued $2K-$3K monthly spend. This is a severe, active capital bleed.

recommendations.impact: Immediately halt spend on the 22 zero-revenue Bing campaigns to prevent further capital bleed, reallocating those funds to Google Search.
Combat the steep ROAS drop (13x to 6.5x) by rotating fresh creative into Prospecting_Brand_Campaign_02 to overcome ad fatigue.
Ringfence budget for Search_TM_Campaign_01 heading into Q4, as it drives predictable 6.32x returns and anchors the entire portfolio.
